feature: Onboarding UX overhaul — input visibility fix + quick estimate as default

prompts.description: Fix input field visibility with specific color values
prompts.prompt: In components/onboarding.tsx, the input fields and placeholder text have poor visibility. Apply these EXACT color changes to ALL input fields, select dropdowns, and number inputs throughout the entire onboarding flow: 1) Input background: #FFFFFF (white). 2) Input border: #C8BDB0 (warm gray) — must be clearly visible, use border width of 1.5px or Tailwind 'border' class. 3) Input text color: #1A1A1A (near black) for strong readability. 4) Placeholder text color: #8C8279 (warm medium gray) — visible but clearly lighter than typed text. 5) Focus state border: #4A7C59 (the green accent color already used in the app) with ring or outline. 6) Apply these to EVERY input element in the onboarding — text inputs, number inputs, select dropdowns, radio/checkbox groups. Use inline styles or Tailwind arbitrary values like 'border-[#C8BDB0]' and 'text-[#1A1A1A]' and 'placeholder:text-[#8C8279]'. 7) Do NOT change button colors, card backgrounds, or any other non-input elements. Follow CLAUDE.md guidelines.

prompts.description: Restructure onboarding to make Quick Estimate the default path
prompts.prompt: In components/onboarding.tsx, restructure the onboarding flow so that Quick Estimate is the PRIMARY default path. Currently the detailed body stats (weight, body fat %, muscle mass) is the default. Change this so: 1) The first body stats screen shows Quick Estimate fields: height, weight, age, sex, and a body type selector (slim/average/athletic/heavy). 2) Below the quick estimate form, add a secondary link styled as a text button: 'I have detailed body stats (InBody, smart scale, etc.)' that expands or navigates to the detailed input fields (body fat %, muscle mass). 3) The primary option title should be 'Start with the basics' with description 'A faster setup using height, weight, age, and body type.' 4) The secondary option should say 'Use detailed body stats' with description 'Best if you have body fat or scan-based measurements.' 5) Do NOT frame Quick Estimate as inferior. Use warm, practical copy. 6) Add supportive copy: 'You can refine this later with detailed stats.' Follow CLAUDE.md guidelines.

prompts.description: Update nutrition calculation to support quick estimate inputs
prompts.prompt: In lib/nutrition.ts, ensure the calorie and macro target calculation works with Quick Estimate inputs (height, weight, age, sex, body type) in addition to the existing detailed inputs (body fat %, muscle mass). If body fat % and muscle mass are not provided: 1) Estimate body fat % from the body type selector (slim ~12-18%, average ~18-25%, athletic ~15-20%, heavy ~25-35%, adjusted by sex). 2) Estimate lean body mass from weight and estimated body fat. 3) Use these estimates to calculate calorie and macro targets using the same formulas already in place. 4) Make sure the existing detailed calculation path still works when body fat % and muscle mass ARE provided. 5) Add the 'bodyType' field to the UserProfile type in lib/meal-store.ts if it doesn't exist (options: 'slim' | 'average' | 'athletic' | 'heavy'). Follow CLAUDE.md guidelines.

prompts.description: Add option to upgrade to detailed stats from settings
prompts.prompt: In components/settings-screen.tsx, add an option that lets users upgrade their profile from Quick Estimate to Detailed Body Stats at any time. Requirements: 1) Add a section called 'Refine your targets' or 'Update body stats'. 2) If the user originally used Quick Estimate (no body fat % or muscle mass provided), show a prompt: 'Got InBody or smart scale data? Adding detailed body stats can improve your calorie and macro targets.' 3) Tapping it should open input fields for body fat % and muscle mass. 4) On save, recalculate targets using the detailed formula in lib/nutrition.ts. 5) Use warm, non-pressuring copy — do not make users feel their current setup is wrong. 6) Example copy: 'Your current targets are a solid starting point. Detailed stats can fine-tune them further.' Follow CLAUDE.md guidelines.
